text: The surgeon begins the laparoscopic left mesocolon mobilization by dissecting the inferior mesenteric vein through a peritoneum window, guided by the topography of the inferior mesenteric artery. The dissection reveals the space between the mesocolon and retroperitoneum, with careful attention to avoid nerve damage in the area of the inferior mesenteric artery.

The surgeon enlarges the peritoneal window under the inferior mesenteric vein, visible in the view of a previously fixed cadaver. In the second patient, the dissection begins with the inferior mesenteric vein as the initial step for colonic mobilization. An incision is made in the peritoneum under the vein at the duodenum-jejunal junction, followed by a planned dissection to separate the mesocolon from the surrounding tissue. The peritoneal window is then opened under the inferior mesenteric artery without dissecting the superior hypogastric plexus.

The surgeon continues the procedure by dividing the inferior mesenteric artery, following the prior dissection of the inferior mesenteric vein. This approach aims to protect retroperitoneal structures and prevent traction injuries during the laparoscopic mobilization of the left colon.
